title: Fraud signals — staged low-speed motor collisions
content: The Zava Fraud Investigation team monitors a recurring pattern of staged low-speed rear-end collisions. Common signals: (1) the third-party vehicle has multiple occupants who all subsequently submit personal injury claims for whiplash; (2) the policy was incepted within the last 60 days, often with a backdated start date request; (3) the customer's stated occupation is inconsistent with the time of incident (e.g. claimed 9-5 office worker but incident occurred at 03:00); (4) the third-party shop is not on Zava's preferred network and quotes are 35%+ above market median; (5) telematics data shows repeated braking events on the same stretch of road in the prior week. Recommended action: place claim on a 14-day investigation hold under section 13 of the Insurance Contracts Act, request full telematics download, request occupant statements separately, refer to PI specialist Naomi Park.
